# Reviewer briefing のレイヤー別化と review-prompts の .harness/custom/ への移設

## Summary

Author 7 per-layer briefing files under .harness/custom/review-prompts/ (additive copy/create, CI-green), then perform the atomic clean-move commit as the paired T002+T003+T004 migration (update review-scope.json content + loader paths + test helper + test literals + live docs + delete old files), then run cargo make ci as the final gate.

## Tasks (5/5 resolved)

### S1 — Author new briefing files (additive)

> T001 creates the .harness/custom/review-prompts/ directory and populates all 7 briefing files (plan-artifacts.md copied from track/review-prompts/, plus 6 new layer files). The legacy source file stays in place until T002 deletes the old directory, so this step is purely additive and cargo make ci passes independently. The new briefing files must exist before T002 commits the new briefing_file values in .harness/config/review-scope.json.

- [x] **T001**: Author all 7 briefing files under .harness/custom/review-prompts/. Copy the current contents of track/review-prompts/plan-artifacts.md into .harness/custom/review-prompts/plan-artifacts.md (content preserved verbatim) while leaving the legacy source file in place until T002 deletes the old directory. Create the 6 new per-layer briefings: domain.md / usecase.md / infrastructure.md / cli.md / cli_composition.md / harness-policy.md. Each file must follow the plan-artifacts.md 'What to report / What NOT to report' structure and include layer-specific severity viewpoints sourced from the relevant conventions (domain: type safety / invariants / enum-first / typestate / no-panics per coding-principles.md + prefer-type-safe-abstractions.md; usecase: purity rules / no std::fs|net|process|io|env / no chronological calls / orchestration-only per hexagonal-architecture.md; infrastructure: adapter placement / I-O boundary / serde codec / symlink rejection / trusted-root enforcement; cli: CLI as composition root / usecase-only call path / no domain::| infrastructure:: direct-use in non-DI code; cli_composition: wiring / DI correctness / no application-logic leakage; harness-policy: convention consistency / command wiring integrity / responsibility-boundary adherence). This task is purely additive: no existing files are modified or deleted, no loader paths change. cargo make ci passes independently at this commit boundary. (IN-01 / IN-02 / AC-01 / AC-02 / AC-03 / CN-03 / CN-04) (`7bf7cef7`)

### S2 — Atomic migration: config, loader, tests, docs, old-file deletion

> T002 is the clean-move config/loader portion required by CN-01: it creates .harness/config/review-scope.json (with repointed briefing_file values and harness-policy pattern cleanup), updates both hardcoded loader paths in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/, updates the test helper that writes the scope JSON file so it targets the new path, and deletes track/review-scope.json plus the legacy track/review-prompts/ directory. T003's test literal updates and T004's live-doc reference updates are part of the same atomic migration commit, so CI never sees loader/config changes while tests or live docs still assert old path values. Depends on T001.

- [x] **T002**: Atomic migration commit: (a) Create .harness/config/review-scope.json with the contents of track/review-scope.json plus all briefing_file values repointed to .harness/custom/review-prompts/<scope>.md for all 7 scopes, and the explicit 'track/review-scope.json' entry removed from harness-policy.patterns (it is now covered by the existing '.harness/**' pattern). (b) Update the two hardcoded loader paths: apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/scope.rs line 51 (root.join('track/review-scope.json') → root.join('.harness/config/review-scope.json')) and apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/shared.rs line 179 (canonical_root.join('track/review-scope.json') → canonical_root.join('.harness/config/review-scope.json')). (c) Update the test helper setup_git_repo_with_scope_json in apps/cli/src/commands/review/tests.rs so it writes the scope JSON to .harness/config/review-scope.json (create the .harness/config/ subdirectory) instead of track/review-scope.json, so the production loader finds the file. (d) Delete track/review-scope.json and the legacy track/review-prompts/ directory, including the retained track/review-prompts/plan-artifacts.md source copy from T001. This commit must be paired with T003's test literal updates and T004's live-doc reference updates in the same atomic migration commit required by CN-01 (clean move, no fallback): the loader, config, test helper, test expectations, live docs, and old files must all change together to avoid a broken-CI window. Depends on T001 (the .harness/custom/review-prompts/ files must exist before the new briefing_file values are committed). Do not treat T002 alone as a CI-green boundary; cargo make ci is expected after the paired T002+T003+T004 migration edits and finally in T005.
